source_sequence: other-benchmarks/raw/hotpotqa/hotpot_dev_distractor_v1.json#5a86eee85542994775f60787:Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad
reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify which railroad had a station stop in Ringling, Montana.
2. Using that railroad, determine the years during which it operated.
original_final_question: During what years did the railroad that had a station stop in Ringling, Montana operate? Write your answer as [ANSWER].
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1: Which railroad had a station stop in Ringling, Montana? Evidence: Ringling, Montana: The town was a station stop on the transcontinental main line of the Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad ("the Milwaukee Road"); it was also the southern terminus of the White Sulphur Springs and Yellowstone Park Railway, which ran from Ringling to White Sulphur Springs. -> Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad

Node 2: During what years did the Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad operate? Evidence: Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad: The Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad (often referred to as the Milwaukee Road) (reporting mark MILW) , was a Class I railroad that operated in the Midwest and Northwest of the United States from 1847 until 1980, when its Pacific Extension (Montana, Idaho, and Washington) was abandoned following a bankruptcy. -> from 1847 until 1980
